title: Undercut Pressure Features
summary: F1 pit timing is often reactive: drivers pit to undercut, cover rivals, or avoid traffic.
rationale: F1 pit timing is often reactive: drivers pit to undercut, cover rivals, or avoid traffic. Existing features use own tyre/lap state but little direct rival-pressure context beyond race-lap medians.
implementation_hint: For each Race-LapNumber, sort by Position and derive features for cars ahead/behind: position gap proxy, their TyreLife, Compound, PitStop, LapTime_Delta, and whether a nearby rival just pitted on current or previous lap. Add interactions such as own_old_tyre_vs_ahead_fresh_tyre and behind_on_older_tyre_pressure.
expected_effect: May catch strategic stops that happen before severe tyre degradation because nearby competitors trigger a cover/undercut response.
risk: Position is ordinal, not time gap, so traffic pressure is approximate; current-lap group features must not use PitNextLap.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
